description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly light gray coloration and scattered dark mineral flecks throughout its surface. The stone has a rough, uneven texture with jagged edges and a generally flat underside, suggesting it may have been naturally fractured or broken from a larger rock. One side of the stone features a visible label or marking, indicating it may have been cataloged or collected for study or landscaping purposes. The surface is matte and non-reflective, with no visible signs of polish or artificial treatment. The stone's thickness varies, with one edge appearing notably thinner and more angular than the rest. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park, garden, or roadside setting.
description: A rough, light gray stone with dark flecks and an irregular, jagged shape. It has a matte, unpolished surface and a visible label on one side.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's uneven, jagged top surface and the scattered dark flecks across its light gray body.
description_view_1: From this angle, the stone appears flatter and more elongated, with the rough texture and mineral inclusions clearly visible.
description_view_2: This side view reveals the stone's relatively flat underside and a visible label or marking on the edge, indicating cataloging.
description_view_3: This perspective shows the stone's broad, flat surface and the continuation of the label along the edge, emphasizing its angular profile.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Scattered dark mineral flecks and speckles irregularly distributed across the light gray surface, with no regular or artificial pattern.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with minor surface irregularities suggesting natural weathering but no significant erosion or biological growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts moderately with manicured or paved urban environments, but can blend with natural landscaping or other stones when placed in clusters.
